description: Add Cell 3.5 (Quick Setup Dashboard) and Cell 3.6 (Feature Toggles) as new sections between Cell 3 (line 4135) and Cell 4 (line 4138) in LTX_PRO.py. These provide a user-friendly interface with presets, resolution shortcuts, difficulty levels, and unified enable/disable toggles for all major features.
steps: Insert new Cell 3.5 section after line 4135 (after the line: print("   \u2713 mount_google_drive()      \u2014 Drive persistence helpers")) with section header matching existing style: # \u2550\u2550\u2550...\n# CELL 3.5  \u2500  QUICK SETUP DASHBOARD\n# \u2550\u2550\u2550...

In Cell 3.5, add a PRESET system: GENERATION_PRESET = 'Standard' # @param ["Quick Draft", "Standard", "Cinema Quality", "T4 Safe"] with a dict mapping each preset to optimal values (resolution, frames, LLM model, sampling settings, tiled VAE, etc.)
In Cell 3.5, add RESOLUTION_PRESET = 'Landscape 16:9' # @param ["Portrait 9:16", "Landscape 16:9", "Square 1:1", "Widescreen 21:9", "Custom"] with a dict mapping each to (width, height) tuples
In Cell 3.5, add GENERATION_MODE = 'Single Clip' # @param ["Single Clip", "Storyboard", "Infinite Flow"] that sets USE_STORYBOARD and USE_SEGMENT_EXTENSION accordingly
In Cell 3.5, add DIFFICULTY_LEVEL = 'Intermediate' # @param ["Beginner", "Intermediate", "Expert"] with logic that prints which settings are active/hidden per level
In Cell 3.5, add a print_current_config() function that displays all active settings in a formatted table using print() statements with box-drawing characters

In Cell 3.5, add a validate_config() function that checks for conflicts (e.g., T4 GPU with Cinema preset, incompatible resolution+frames combos, segment extension without enough VRAM) and prints warnings
In Cell 3.5, add an apply_preset() function that applies the selected GENERATION_PRESET by setting global variables, and call it at the end of the cell
Insert new Cell 3.6 section after Cell 3.5 with header: # CELL 3.6  \u2500  FEATURE TOGGLES

In Cell 3.6, add all ENABLE_* toggle variables with @param {type:"boolean"} annotations: ENABLE_VIDEO_GENERATION, ENABLE_UPSCALING, ENABLE_AUDIO_GENERATION, ENABLE_CHARACTER_SYSTEM, ENABLE_POST_PROCESSING, ENABLE_QUALITY_CHECKS, ENABLE_LLM_PROMPT_EXPANSION, ENABLE_VISION_ANALYSIS, ENABLE_SCENE_CONTINUITY, ENABLE_SEGMENT_EXTENSION
In Cell 3.6, add disable_all_extras() function that sets all ENABLE_* toggles to False except ENABLE_VIDEO_GENERATION
In Cell 3.6, add enable_recommended() function that reads _VRAM_MGR to detect GPU and enables appropriate features (fewer for T4, more for A100)
In Cell 3.6, add the Interactive Helper Functions: show_help(topic), estimate_generation_time(), estimate_vram_usage(), suggest_settings(goal)
End both Cell 3.5 and Cell 3.6 with summary print() statements showing what's enabled/disabled, matching existing style
Update the CELL ORDER docstring at the top of the file (around line 28) to include Cell 3.5 and Cell 3.6
acceptance_criteria: python3 -m py_compile LTX_PRO.py passes without errors
All new variables use @param {type:".."} Colab annotation format
Section headers use box-drawing characters matching existing Cell headers
print_current_config() produces a readable formatted table
validate_config() detects at least 3 types of conflicts and prints warnings
GENERATION_PRESET with all 4 options maps to concrete settings
All 10 ENABLE_* toggles are defined with sensible defaults
disable_all_extras() and enable_recommended() modify the correct globals
estimate_generation_time() returns a time estimate based on resolution and frames
estimate_vram_usage() returns a VRAM estimate based on current settings
